mend_config.rationale: Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool list. scanSettings.versioning cannot pin a Bun toolchain. No .whitesource file is emitted — detection is lockfile-driven (bun.lock JSONC parsing).

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver (js-bun is Bucket C in whitesource-config.md). No .whitesource file is emitted — manual tool version pinning via scanSettings.versioning is impossible for this ecosystem. Detection is lockfile-driven (bun.lock JSONC parsing). Reproducibility guarantee is reduced.

BUNFIG.TOML SIGNAL: The [install.scopes] block in bunfig.toml maps '@myorg' to 'https://npm.example.com/'. Mend MUST read bunfig.toml and apply this mapping to classify @myorg/private-utils as source: 'private-index'. If Mend ignores bunfig.toml, both packages fall back to source: 'registry' — that is the primary failure mode this probe is designed to expose.

SCOPED PACKAGE FAILURE MODE: If Mend does not read bunfig.toml or does not apply [install.scopes], @myorg/private-utils will be reported as source: 'registry' with registry: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'. That is wrong — the package is from the private registry at npm.example.com.

CONTROL PACKAGE INVARIANT: hono must report source: 'registry' with registry: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'. If Mend misapplies the scoped registry rule to ALL packages (not just @myorg/* scoped ones), hono would incorrectly report source: 'private-index'. That is a distinct bug from the primary failure mode.

STUB PACKAGE NOTE: @myorg/private-utils@1.0.0 does not exist on the public npm registry. npm.example.com is a fictional registry URL. The lockfile entry uses a placeholder integrity hash. The Mend scanner should parse the lockfile entry as-recorded, regardless of registry reachability during scan time.

SOURCE TYPE MAPPING: bunfig.toml [install.scopes] registry assignment maps to Mend source type 'private-index'. This is distinct from 'registry' (public npm), 'git' (git+https / github:), 'url' (tarball URL), and 'local' (file:/link:). The code path for reading bunfig.toml is separate from manifest-side source detection.

Per open question Q4 in docs/BUN_COVERAGE_PLAN.md §9: 'Does Mend read bunfig.toml?' This probe is the designated instrument to answer that question. If the scan reports @myorg/private-utils as source: 'registry', the answer is No and a Mend bug should be filed.

Related probe: bun-basic-registry-probe (Tier 1 #1) exercises the basic registry path without bunfig.toml. Cross-compare: if both probes report source: 'registry' for hono, the hono detection path is stable. If this probe additionally reports @myorg/private-utils as source: 'registry', bunfig.toml reading is confirmed broken.
